decisions.title: Attend AI Innovation Summit with DeepMind at Google I/O
decisions.context: Strategic opportunity to understand AI trends that could inform Ask SIFT and Canvas evolution. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar.

arguments.for: Direct insight into cutting-edge AI capabilities; networking with DeepMind could accelerate our AI product roadmap
arguments.against: Time investment during Q2 sprint; unclear immediate ROI for current product deliverables

decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 Legend Consistency (Scatter, GeoMap, Histogram)
decisions.context: Three parallel legend improvement tickets suggest systemic UX debt in Explore V2. Impacts 'Nail the Core' pillar and user experience consistency.

arguments.for: Unified legend system reduces cognitive load; improves product polish; enables faster feature development
arguments.against: Resource allocation away from new feature development; not directly tied to North Star metric

decisions.title: Expand Global Nav IA Testing to Include Edge Users
decisions.context: DES-156/158 plans 15 sessions but signal doesn't confirm Edge user inclusion. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' as Edge expands.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear if Edge users are in testing cohort; may need recruitment extension

arguments.for: Edge represents growing user segment with unique workflows; IA must accommodate field technician patterns
arguments.against: Delays current testing timeline; Edge usage patterns still evolving

decisions.title: Accelerate Python Exec Alarms for Production Readiness
decisions.context: Python Rules is key 2026 product. Alarms are critical for production trust. Supports 'Nail the Core' and decision capture.

arguments.for: Enables customers to trust Python Rules in critical workflows; directly increases decisions captured
arguments.against: May require pulling engineers from Canvas or Edge work

decisions.title: Define Conversation State Machine Scope for Ask SIFT
decisions.context: ENG-10385 is foundational architecture. Unclear scope could delay Ask SIFT GA. Impacts 'Build the Moat' pillar.

arguments.for: Clear state machine enables predictable conversational AI; unblocks follow-up features
arguments.against: Risk of over-engineering early; may need to iterate based on user feedback

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination week with UXR kickoff and FedRAMP sync. Limited deep work windows.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks scheduled today - recommend blocking Tue/Thu mornings
3 one-on-ones on same day reduces context switching time
22 meetings this week is 38% above team average - consider async alternatives
